narrative.summary: You're on College near Bathurst, looking for an unmarked door that requires an Instagram password to enter—yes, really. Inside, low lighting catches brass fixtures and dark wood, with jazz threading through at a volume that doesn't interrupt conversation. The bartender trained at Le Cordon Bleu and you can tell—these aren't drinks that announce themselves.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on College near Bathurst, looking for an unmarked door that requires an Instagram password to enter—yes, really. Inside, low lighting catches brass fixtures and dark wood, with jazz filling the narrow room and a bar where a Le Cordon Bleu-trained bartender builds drinks like compositions. It's deliberate speakeasy theater, but the hospitality is warm enough that you forget you're playing along.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is a cocktail bar first, with a tight menu built to complement the drink program. The pretzel comes with maple butter that's become something of a signature—sweet, salty, worth ordering even if you're not hungry. There's a steakhouse downstairs (a popular pairing according to regulars), but up here it's about small, considered bites that don't compete with the culinary-driven cocktails, many with house-made elements and enough invention to justify the speakeasy hunt.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: This is a post-dinner move, ideally when you want something more considered than Little Italy's wine bar crush. Come late on a weeknight when you can actually talk to the bartender, or after steak downstairs if you're committing to the full ritual.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "A hidden gem with a speakeasy ambiance. The hospitality is welcoming, and the cocktail program is thoughtful and inventive.

Having great steak downstairs and finished the night upstairs with delicious, well-crafted cocktails. Definitely recommend this combo!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Great little hideaway - be sure to check their Instagram for that night's code!

Fantastic cocktails, exceptionally well-made, friendly service. The pretzel with the maple butter was delightful. Will definitely be back when I'm next in the area!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "This cocktail bar has only been open for a month, I anticipate it will get more busy given how great the cocktails are (Little Italy has no shortage of cocktail bars). Really inventive cocktails, a lot of elements made in-house. I also ordered a custom cocktail which I really liked.
They have expensive champagne bottles if that's your thing 🤑
Very chic lounge atmosphere.
Food wise, there's mostly sweet items (they have a pastry chef!) so wouldn't recommend this if you're looking for bar bites too."
